title: Feature: hex as first-class citizen inside opencode

phases.name: hex-nexus: OpenAI-compatible inference proxy
phases.description: Add GET /v1/models and POST /v1/chat/completions to hex-nexus. These proxy to the registered inference provider selected by model prefix (e.g. 'ollama/llama3' → Ollama endpoint). Satisfies S04, S05, S07.

tasks.name: Add GET /v1/models route to hex-nexus
tasks.description: Add route handler in hex-nexus/src/routes/inference.rs (or a new openai_proxy.rs). GET /v1/models returns registered providers as model list in OpenAI format: { object: 'list', data: [{ id: '<provider>/<model>', object: 'model', owned_by: 'hex-nexus' }] }. Read provider list from the inference providers table via AppState.

tasks.name: Add POST /v1/chat/completions proxy route to hex-nexus
tasks.description: Add POST /v1/chat/completions route. Parse incoming OpenAI-format body. Extract provider from model field prefix (e.g. 'hex-nexus/claude-sonnet' → strip prefix, use default provider; 'ollama/llama3' → route to ollama). Forward to the appropriate registered provider's /v1/chat/completions endpoint using existing inference routing logic. Return the provider's response as-is (both streaming SSE and non-streaming JSON). Security: reject model strings that don't map to a registered provider (S07).

phases.name: hex-cli: skills injection into opencode.json commands
phases.description: Expand write_opencode_config() in hex-cli/src/commands/chat.rs to read embedded skills from hex-cli/assets/skills/ (via rust-embed Assets), parse frontmatter, and inject as opencode command entries. Project .claude/skills/ overrides embedded on collision. Satisfies S01, S02, S08, S09.

tasks.name: Parse skill frontmatter and body from embedded assets
tasks.description: In chat.rs, add function load_skills() -> Vec<SkillEntry> where SkillEntry = { name, description, template }. Use crate::assets::Assets to iterate embedded skill files from the 'skills/' prefix. Parse YAML frontmatter (--- block) to extract 'name', 'description', 'trigger' fields. The body after the closing --- is the template. Fall through gracefully on missing fields (S08). Escape handled automatically by serde_json (S09).

tasks.name: Merge project .claude/skills/ overrides
tasks.description: After loading embedded skills, scan CWD/.claude/skills/*.md and ~/.claude/skills/*.md. Parse same frontmatter format. Insert into skill map with project-local overriding embedded (S02). Return merged map keyed by skill name (without leading /).

tasks.name: Inject skills as opencode command entries in opencode.json
tasks.description: In write_opencode_config(), call load_skills(). Build serde_json command map: { skill_name: { template, description } }. Merge into the opencode.json config object alongside existing instructions and mcp fields. If load_skills() returns empty (e.g. assets unavailable), skip the command field — do not emit empty object.

phases.name: hex-cli: agent injection into opencode.json agents
phases.description: Read embedded agent YAML definitions, convert to opencode AgentConfig format, inject as agent entries in opencode.json. Satisfies S03.

tasks.name: Parse agent YAMLs from embedded assets and convert to opencode AgentConfig
tasks.description: Add function load_agents() -> serde_json::Value in chat.rs. Iterate embedded files from 'agents/hex/hex/' prefix via Assets. For each YAML: parse with serde_yaml (already in Cargo.toml or add it). Extract fields: name, description, model.preferred (map to opencode model string e.g. 'anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-6'), constraints array (join as prompt prefix). Build opencode AgentConfig: { model, prompt, description, mode: 'all' }. Return as JSON object keyed by agent name.

tasks.name: Inject agent configs into opencode.json
tasks.description: In write_opencode_config(), call load_agents(). Add agent field to the opencode.json config object. Skip if empty. The hex-coder, planner, hex-reviewer etc. should appear in opencode's agent picker.

phases.name: hex-cli: provider injection for hex-nexus inference routing
phases.description: Inject hex-nexus as an opencode provider in opencode.json. Fetches nexus URL from env/default. Only injected when nexus is reachable. Satisfies S04, S06.

tasks.name: Inject provider.hex into opencode.json pointing at hex-nexus
tasks.description: In write_opencode_config(), after ensuring nexus is reachable (existing ensure_running check), add provider field to opencode.json: { 'hex': { 'api': 'openai', 'name': 'hex-nexus', 'options': { 'baseURL': '<nexus_url>' } } }. The nexus_url is already available in the function. If nexus is unreachable, omit the provider field entirely (S06 graceful degradation). Also set 'model' field in opencode.json to 'hex/<default_model>' if a default provider is configured.

phases.name: ADR update: mark P3-P5 complete in ADR-2603231800
phases.description: Update ADR-2603231800 implementation table to mark the new phases complete.

gate.command: cargo check -p hex-cli && cargo check -p hex-nexus

tasks.name: Update ADR-2603231800 implementation phases
tasks.description: Update docs/adrs/ADR-2603231800-opencode-mcp-injection.md: mark P3 (Agent definitions), P4 (Skills injector), P5 (Provider/inference) as Complete with commit hashes. Add note about opencode command field for skills and AgentConfig for agents.

cargo.name: serde_yaml
cargo.purpose: Parse agent YAML definitions in hex-cli

riskRegister.risk: serde_yaml not in hex-cli Cargo.toml — adds a dependency
riskRegister.mitigation: serde_yaml is lightweight; alternatively parse the YAML manually since we only need 3-4 fields

riskRegister.risk: opencode rejects unknown provider API type 'openai'
riskRegister.mitigation: Check opencode provider list output to confirm correct api field value; may need 'openai-compatible'
Pass SSE chunks through as-is from upstream provider; only transform non-streaming path

successCriteria: hex chat launches opencode with /hex-adr-create and other skills visible as slash commands
hex-coder, planner, hex-reviewer agents appear in opencode's agent picker
Selecting the hex provider in opencode routes inference through hex-nexus
hex chat still launches successfully when nexus is offline (graceful degradation)
